activity_or_event_description.describe_the_activity_or_event_and_the_l:
We are applying for a commercial use permit to operate small-group, guided hiking and nature photography outings within DuPont State Recreational Forest, primarily focused on waterfall and fall-foliage experiences for out-of-town visitors. Our company, Blue Ridge Adventure Guides LLC, is a locally owned guiding service based in Asheville, NC, specializing in low-impact, educational outdoor experiences for adults and families.

The specific activity proposed is a half-day guided hiking and photo-tour called “Waterfalls & Forest Light – DuPont Explorer.” Each outing will be approximately 4–5 hours in duration and will include:

1) A safety briefing at the trailhead (including Leave No Trace principles, trail etiquette around other user groups such as mountain bikers and equestrians, and specific guidance on staying behind protective railings near waterfalls).
2) A moderate hike on established, designated trails to several of DuPont’s well-known scenic features, with frequent stops for photography, natural history interpretation, and short rest breaks.
3) A structured photography and observation segment near one major waterfall where guests are guided on how to frame shots without leaving the trail or trampling vegetation.
4) A return hike via the same established trails back to the designated access area.

Primary locations within the forest we wish to use include:

- Hooker Falls Access Area: We plan to start many of our tours from the Hooker Falls parking area due to its good facilities and clear signage. From there, we will use existing trails to visit Hooker Falls and Triple Falls overlooks. For groups with adequate fitness and time, we may also continue on to the High Falls overlook, staying on marked trails at all times.

- High Falls Access Area: On days when Hooker Falls parking is at capacity, or for slightly more advanced groups, we plan to begin from the High Falls Access Area. From this trailhead, we will use the main multi-use trails to reach the High Falls and Triple Falls viewing areas, remaining on designated viewing platforms and existing hardened surfaces.

- Lake Imaging Access Area (limited use): For a small number of outings, especially in peak fall color season, we may begin at Lake Imaging to offer a quieter forest and lake-edge photography experience. In these cases, we will utilize only established multi-use trails and will not leave designated paths.

Group structure and logistics:
- Participants will be transported to the forest in their own personal vehicles or via pre-arranged carpooling. We will not be operating shuttle buses or large vans inside the forest.
- Each group will be led by one professional guide who holds current Wilderness First Aid certification and CPR training. For groups larger than 8 participants, we will add a second assistant guide to help manage pace and safety.
- We will limit our group size on foot to a maximum of 10 paying participants plus up to 2 guides (12 people total) to minimize crowding on trails and at viewpoints.

Educational and interpretive component:
- During the hikes, we will provide interpretive talks on DuPont’s natural and cultural history, including the geology that creates the waterfalls, the forest’s mixed hardwood ecosystem, seasonal wildflowers, and basic wildlife awareness.
- The photography element is focused on helping visitors capture better landscape and waterfall images using whatever camera they have (smartphones or DSLR). We will explicitly instruct guests not to enter the water at the top of waterfalls, not to cross protective railings, and not to climb on slick rocks.

Safety and environmental protections:
- All tours will follow established trails only; we will not create social trails or lead guests off-trail.
- We will carry a small first-aid kit, extra water, and a printed map of DuPont State Recreational Forest on each outing.
- We will strictly enforce a pack-in/pack-out policy for all trash, including food wrappers and tissues. Guides will perform a brief sweep of rest areas and viewing points before leaving to ensure no litter is left behind by our group.
- We will avoid particularly busy times at the main waterfall viewpoints when possible (e.g., mid-day on peak fall-color weekends) and adjust our timing or route to reduce congestion and user conflict.

Overall, this is a recurring, low-impact, guided hiking and photography program aimed at visitors who want a structured, safe, and educational way to experience DuPont’s scenery. We are not proposing any races, competitive events, amplified music, staging areas beyond normal trailhead use, or any temporary structures. All activities will be completed within daylight hours, and we will comply with all posted rules and any additional conditions attached to the commercial use permit.

users_number_total.users: Same as on-foot users (no driving tours)

insurance_information.liability_insurance_company_name: Blue Ridge Outdoor Risk & Liability Insurance Co.
